Employees.question: Return all employees with employeeid, firstname, lastname, and email, ordered by lastname then firstname.
Employees.answer: select employeeid, firstname, lastname, email from employees order by lastname asc, firstname asc;

Employees.question: Return firstname, lastname, salary for employees with salary greater than 80000. Order by salary descending.
Employees.answer: select firstname, lastname, salary from employees where salary > 80000 order by salary desc;

Employees.question: Return the total number of employees as a single value named employeecount.
Employees.answer: select count(*) as employeecount from employees;

Employees.question: Return firstname, lastname, departmentname, and location for all employees who have a department assigned.
Employees.answer: select e.firstname, e.lastname, d.departmentname, d.location from employees e join departments d on d.departmentid = e.departmentid;

Employees.question: Return departmentname and average salary (alias avgsalary) for each department. Only include departments with average salary greater than 70000. Order by avgsalary descending.
Employees.answer: select d.departmentname, avg(e.salary) as avgsalary from employees e join departments d on d.departmentid = e.departmentid group by d.departmentid, d.departmentname having avg(e.salary) > 70000 order by avgsalary desc;

Employees.question: Return employees who do not have a matching department record. Show employeeid, firstname, lastname.
Employees.answer: select e.employeeid, e.firstname, e.lastname from employees e left join departments d on d.departmentid = e.departmentid where d.departmentid is null;

Employees.question: Return employees whose salary is greater than the average salary of their own department. Show firstname, lastname, salary, departmentid.
Employees.answer: select e.firstname, e.lastname, e.salary, e.departmentid from employees e where e.departmentid is not null and e.salary > (select avg(e2.salary) from employees e2 where e2.departmentid = e.departmentid);

Employees.question: Return departments where total salary expense exceeds the department budget. Show departmentname, budget, totalsalary.
Employees.answer: select d.departmentname, d.budget, sum(e.salary) as totalsalary from departments d join employees e on e.departmentid = d.departmentid group by d.departmentid, d.departmentname, d.budget having sum(e.salary) > d.budget;

Employees.question: Return the top 2 highest-paid employees in each department. Show departmentid, firstname, lastname, salary.
Employees.answer: select departmentid, firstname, lastname, salary from (select e.departmentid, e.firstname, e.lastname, e.salary, row_number() over (partition by e.departmentid order by e.salary desc) as rn from employees e where e.departmentid is not null) ranked where rn <= 2 order by departmentid asc, salary desc;

Employees.question: Select all employees
Employees.answer: SELECT * FROM Employees;

Employees.question: Get employee first and last names
Employees.answer: SELECT firstname, lastname FROM Employees;

Employees.question: Count total employees
Employees.answer: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Employees;

Employees.question: Get all unique department IDs from employees
Employees.answer: SELECT DISTINCT departmentId FROM Employees;

Employees.question: Get employees ordered by last name
Employees.answer: SELECT * FROM Employees ORDER BY lastname ASC;

Employees.question: Get top 5 highest paid employees
Employees.answer: SELECT * FROM Employees ORDER BY salary DESC LIMIT 5;

Employees.question: Get employees with salary greater than 80000
Employees.answer: SELECT * FROM Employees WHERE salary > 80000;

Employees.question: Get employees in department 3
Employees.answer: SELECT * FROM Employees WHERE departmentId = 3;

Employees.question: Get average employee salary
Employees.answer: SELECT AVG(salary) FROM Employees;

Employees.question: Get employees whose first name starts with A
Employees.answer: SELECT * FROM Employees WHERE firstname LIKE 'A%';

Employees.question: Get the highest salary in the company
Employees.answer: SELECT MAX(salary) FROM Employees;

Employees.question: Get the lowest salary in the company
Employees.answer: SELECT MIN(salary) FROM Employees;

Employees.question: Get total salary expense
Employees.answer: SELECT SUM(salary) FROM Employees;

Employees.question: Get number of employees per department
Employees.answer: SELECT departmentId, COUNT(*) AS total FROM Employees GROUP BY departmentId;

Employees.question: Get employees with email containing company
Employees.answer: SELECT * FROM Employees WHERE email LIKE '%company%';

Departments.question: Get department names and locations ordered by departmentname.
Departments.answer: select departmentname, location from departments order by departmentname asc;

Departments.question: Count total departments as departmentcount.
Departments.answer: select count(*) as departmentcount from departments;

Departments.question: Get departments ordered by name
Departments.answer: SELECT * FROM Departments ORDER BY departmentName ASC;

Departments.question: Get employees with their department name
Departments.answer: SELECT e.firstname, e.lastname, d.departmentName FROM Employees e JOIN Departments d ON e.departmentId = d.departmentId;

Departments.question: Get average salary per department
Departments.answer: SELECT d.departmentName, AVG(e.salary) AS avg_salary FROM Employees e JOIN Departments d ON e.departmentId = d.departmentId GROUP BY d.departmentName;

Departments.question: Get departments with more than 3 employees
Departments.answer: SELECT d.departmentName, COUNT(e.employeeId) AS total FROM Departments d JOIN Employees e ON d.departmentId = e.departmentId GROUP BY d.departmentName HAVING COUNT(e.employeeId) > 3;

Departments.question: Get departments with no employees
Departments.answer: SELECT * FROM Departments WHERE departmentId NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT departmentId FROM Employees);

Departments.question: Get the department with the highest total salary
Departments.answer: SELECT d.departmentName, SUM(e.salary) AS total FROM Departments d JOIN Employees e ON d.departmentId = e.departmentId GROUP BY d.departmentName ORDER BY total DESC LIMIT 1;

Employees_Departments.question: Get each employee with their department name
Employees_Departments.answer: SELECT e.firstname, e.lastname, d.departmentName FROM Employees e INNER JOIN Departments d ON e.departmentId = d.departmentId;

Employees_Departments.question: Get all departments and their employees including departments with no employees
Employees_Departments.answer: SELECT d.departmentName, e.firstname, e.lastname FROM Departments d LEFT JOIN Employees e ON d.departmentId = e.departmentId;

Employees_Departments.question: Get all employees including those with no department
Employees_Departments.answer: SELECT e.firstname, e.lastname, d.departmentName FROM Employees e LEFT JOIN Departments d ON e.departmentId = d.departmentId;

Employees_Departments.question: Get average salary per department name
Employees_Departments.answer: SELECT d.departmentName, AVG(e.salary) AS avg_salary FROM Employees e JOIN Departments d ON e.departmentId = d.departmentId GROUP BY d.departmentName;

Employees_Departments.question: Get departments with more than 3 employees
Employees_Departments.answer: SELECT d.departmentName, COUNT(e.employeeId) AS total FROM Departments d JOIN Employees e ON d.departmentId = e.departmentId GROUP BY d.departmentName HAVING COUNT(e.employeeId) > 3;

Employees_Departments.question: Get the highest paid employee in each department
Employees_Departments.answer: SELECT d.departmentName, e.firstname, e.lastname, e.salary FROM Employees e JOIN Departments d ON e.departmentId = d.departmentId WHERE e.salary = (SELECT MAX(salary) FROM Employees WHERE departmentId = e.departmentId);

Employees_Departments.question: Get total salary cost per department
Employees_Departments.answer: SELECT d.departmentName, SUM(e.salary) AS total_salary FROM Departments d JOIN Employees e ON d.departmentId = e.departmentId GROUP BY d.departmentName ORDER BY total_salary DESC;

Employees_Departments.question: Get employees who earn more than their department average
Employees_Departments.answer: SELECT e.firstname, e.lastname, e.salary FROM Employees e WHERE e.salary > (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM Employees WHERE departmentId = e.departmentId);

Customers.question: Return customerid, firstname, lastname, city for customers in Canada, ordered by lastname then firstname.
Customers.answer: select customerid, firstname, lastname, city from customers where lower(country) = 'canada' order by lastname asc, firstname asc;

Customers.question: Return the total number of orders as ordercount.
Customers.answer: select count(*) as ordercount from orders;

Customers.question: Return customers who have never placed an order. Show customerid, firstname, lastname.
Customers.answer: select c.customerid, c.firstname, c.lastname from customers c left join orders o on o.customerid = c.customerid where o.orderid is null;

Customers.question: Get customer first and last names
Get customers ordered by last name
Customers.answer: SELECT * FROM Customers ORDER BY lastname ASC;

Customers.question: Get distinct countries customers are from
Get customers whose last name contains son
Customers.answer: SELECT * FROM Customers WHERE lastname LIKE '%son%';

Customers.question: Get customers from a specific city Toronto
Count customers per country
Customers.answer: SELECT country, COUNT(*) AS total FROM Customers GROUP BY country;

Orders.question: Return orderid, orderdate, totalamount for orders with totalamount >= 1000, ordered by orderdate descending.
Orders.answer: select orderid, orderdate, totalamount from orders where totalamount >= 1000 order by orderdate desc;

Orders.question: Return orderid, orderdate, customer full name as customername, and totalamount for all orders.
Orders.answer: select o.orderid, o.orderdate, c.firstname || ' ' || c.lastname as customername, o.totalamount from orders o join customers c on c.customerid = o.customerid;

Orders.question: Return customerid and total spend (alias totalspent) for each customer with totalspent > 2000. Order by totalspent descending.
Orders.answer: select c.customerid, sum(o.totalamount) as totalspent from customers c join orders o on o.customerid = c.customerid group by c.customerid having sum(o.totalamount) > 2000 order by totalspent desc;

Orders.question: Return city and number of orders (alias ordersincity) for each city, ordered by ordersincity descending.
Orders.answer: select c.city, count(o.orderid) as ordersincity from customers c join orders o on o.customerid = c.customerid group by c.city order by ordersincity desc;

Orders.question: Return each customerid with their most recent orderdate and totalamount. Include all rows matching the most recent date.
Orders.answer: select o.customerid, o.orderdate, o.totalamount from orders o where o.orderdate = (select max(o2.orderdate) from orders o2 where o2.customerid = o.customerid);

Orders.question: Return monthly totals with the previous month total (alias prevtotal) and difference (alias diff). Month format YYYY-MM, ordered by month.
Orders.answer: with monthly as (select strftime('%Y-%m', orderdate) as month, sum(totalamount) as total from orders group by strftime('%Y-%m', orderdate)) select month, total, lag(total) over (order by month) as prevtotal, total - lag(total) over (order by month) as diff from monthly order by month;

Orders.question: Select all orders
Orders.answer: SELECT * FROM Orders;

Orders.question: Get total number of orders
Orders.answer: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Orders;

Orders.question: Get orders ordered by date descending
Orders.answer: SELECT * FROM Orders ORDER BY orderDate DESC;

Orders.question: Get total revenue from all orders
Orders.answer: SELECT SUM(totalAmount) FROM Orders;

Orders.question: Get average order amount
Orders.answer: SELECT AVG(totalAmount) FROM Orders;

Orders.question: Get orders with amount greater than 100
Orders.answer: SELECT * FROM Orders WHERE totalAmount > 100;

Orders.question: Get orders placed in 2025
Orders.answer: SELECT * FROM Orders WHERE orderDate BETWEEN '2025-01-01' AND '2025-12-31';

Orders.question: Get number of orders per customer
Orders.answer: SELECT customerId, COUNT(*) AS total_orders FROM Orders GROUP BY customerId;

Orders.question: Get customers with their orders
Orders.answer: SELECT c.firstname, c.lastname, o.orderId, o.totalAmount FROM Customers c JOIN Orders o ON c.customerId = o.customerId;

Orders.question: Get customers who have more than 1 order
Orders.answer: SELECT customerId, COUNT(*) AS total FROM Orders GROUP BY customerId HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;

Orders.question: Get the highest order amount
Orders.answer: SELECT MAX(totalAmount) FROM Orders;

Orders.question: Get the customer who spent the most
Orders.answer: SELECT customerId, SUM(totalAmount) AS total_spent FROM Orders GROUP BY customerId ORDER BY total_spent DESC LIMIT 1;

Orders.question: Get orders with zero amount
Orders.answer: SELECT * FROM Orders WHERE totalAmount = 0;

Orders.question: Get total revenue per customer
Orders.answer: SELECT c.firstname, c.lastname, SUM(o.totalAmount) AS revenue FROM Customers c JOIN Orders o ON c.customerId = o.customerId GROUP BY c.customerId;

Orders.question: Get orders sorted by amount descending
Orders.answer: SELECT * FROM Orders ORDER BY totalAmount DESC;
